title: oxalis neglecta
intro: Oxalis neglecta, also known as the woodsorrel or sourgrass, is a type of flowering plant belonging to the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). Traditionally, the plant's leaves have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including their high concentration of oxalic acid, which has been used to treat conditions such as rheumatism and skin irritations. The health benefits of Oxalis neglecta include its potential anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and digestive issues. Oxalis neglecta is often prepared as a herbal tea or infused in water to utilize its medicinal properties, although its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity in high concentrations.
